speaker: MATT
raw_text: You did hear all that. Okay, and I'd say, as they're making their way out, you can probably duck around without them seeing you as they left. Yeah, you can do that. So, you guys are jettisoned into the street. It's now probably just past midnight.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Her accent is really strong! It's hard to not match her accent! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's the one accent that I really have trouble with.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Would you call it infectious?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Bastard.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The one that we know is a problem.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to call my cat back to me and I drop the telepathy and I just start tugging you back to the Nestled Nook. Come on, we have to talk about this. We have to decide what we're going to do.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Right away.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Excuse me--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They're not with us.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I thought you were right behind us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: My cat was in there, but not I.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but you'd have to stay within 100 feet to maintain that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, we would've walked up to the building, the cat would've scurried around, if possible. So I'd like to think that I heard all that, which is why just I said all the things I did.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At this point, a man who is on the far end of the hall walks up. And you can see him, he's a man with a heavy, bushy beard that obscures a lot of a dark leather mantle, over a general cloth tunic. He has burly arms, heavy torso, super scrawny legs, a widow's peak, and he looks to be the jailer. He walks up and puts his hand on the bars and goes, "Oh well, if you want to be patient, I'm sure the Lawmaster will be here sometime in the morning and you can discuss whatever this business is. But if you want to say goodbye, now's the time."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Are they not going to take her to see the Lawmaker?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know, why doesn't she get to talk to the Lawmaster?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As he closes and locks it, "Because she decided not to come helpfully."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What do you mean?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ah, oh, no you misunderstood. See that was just, I have this spasm that has this twitch. It was an injury from, like, a childhood thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no, are you guys making fun of her because of her spasm?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah that's actually-- (fake crying) it was very sensitive for me. I had a really tough childhood. I was just trying to find a bathroom.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The three guards walk away, not caring. Who were there previously and--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Spasm shaming.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Yeah, they came in not but a few minutes before you did. They went upstairs."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, your friends!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Can we look around and see if we spot Caleb and--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You call them Stinky and the little one? All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You spot around the interior of the tavern. They don't appear to be nearby.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I wonder if they came back.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'll go up and ask the tavern keeper.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Yorda?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, Yorda.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, hi! Everything all right? You had a few bruiser types leading you in here, some of the crownsguard, huh?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my gosh, there was this big trouble at the carnival, did you go?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I heard about that. I didn't go in there, but people are coming in here saying--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Somebody turned into a zombie and then--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I heard that! That's incredible!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! And then we're all under investigation because we saved them! These guards are weird. Hey, did you see the wizard guy, the stinky guy and the little halfling?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Being about one o'clock in the morning at this time, and the energy in the tavern has come to a quiet crawl, those who are keeping their cups are either snoring or in the process of preparing to go to sleep, you watch as Adelaine, the barmaid is walking around cleaning up tables and suddenly all the windows in the room slam open with a heavy impact and there's a burst of wind that comes through. The two crownsguard that were outside flanking the opening doorway suddenly rush and look in and look about at the lot of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How do you come by learning Halfling?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: My parents were in the winery business. The wine making business. And halflings really like wine.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Everyone likes wine!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's true.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Some people more than others.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Some halflings just deal in wine a lot, and a lot did in the town that I'm from and then my parents wanted me to keep up with the books and all that stuff so it forced me to learn halfling. I was around it a lot.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What town was that then?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's close by here, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's a bit aways. It's Kamordah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Hmm, haven't heard of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's a small farming town, it's no big deal. It's whatever.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Nothing shady there!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Nope, just a wandering traveler just like the rest of y'all.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well I mean I was really impressed with the little trick, I sure would like to see a big trick.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I use Thaumaturgy to open all the windows!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All righty. Fairly decent sketches, actually. You eventually find yourself to sleep. The rest of you, go to your respective rooms and find yourselves falling into a night's rest. As evening comes to a close and the morning brings you to consciousness, you are rested. You are healed up, your spells are restored.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Where exactly am I going to be sleeping, by the way? I can sleep down here, but it might be nice--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Molly, if you would like to share my room, you can.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Excellent. You don't mind? I'm going to pull the swords off finally and start getting comfortable out of the performance clothes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm in Jester's room, still, I think. Sleeping on the floor on my bedroll.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: As she starts to go to sleep, I want to draw in my sketchbook and tell the Traveler all about the cool things I saw for the day. I'm going to draw the red faced guard as I pretended I was a zombie, and I'm going to draw Molly's horns all full of jewels and stuff! I'm going to draw the stack of books.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The monster attack?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And I'm going to draw like a zombie, but like as an old man but as he's biting his dentures fall out and he's not no teeth!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Coming to consciousness in your respective rooms, cold morning air. It's a bit frosty. The windows to each of your rooms have a bit of that curled bit of frost on the outskirts of the glass. It's probably 8:00 or 9:00 in the morning at this point in time and the day is yours. What do you wish to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. Fairly decent sketches, actually. You eventually find yourself to sleep. The rest of you, go to your respective rooms and find yourselves falling into a night's rest. As evening comes to a close and the morning brings you to consciousness, you are rested. You are healed up, your spells are restored.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I can use the Long Rest feature of D&D Beyond!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You hit a button!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And all my spells back!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There is a button for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Whoa, nothing changed! No spells. I lost ten hit points?! What the fuck?! I've done something terribly wrong. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: D&D Beyond's watching going, "Fuck you, Nott!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait no, you're putting temporary hit points on! What are you doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll work on this during the break.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Self destruct in 10, nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He just throws the iPad.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, the two of you come to consciousness. What are you up to?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Hey, Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you have anything you can do to make yourself not look like you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I take my robes and I flip them inside out and I take some dirt and dirty it up a little bit and take my hair down and I steal a scarf off of Jester's stuff and I do a bandana around my head.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, you look like you with a bandana around your head.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah that's not going to cut it, not quite like what we had in mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What are you guys doing? You guys leaving? Are you guys fucking leaving? You're not leaving without me are you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, of course, we're coming back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You're skipping town.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. Oh, but we could.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: She's right here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I could skip town with you, though. Do you want to skip town?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We'll be back.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's see if Caleb and his little friend are downstairs first. He has tricks after all.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Right.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "There's nobody nearby. He had a son about 35 years ago, but he got married and went up north."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It's this very whiskey you just had."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll take one more, and I'll get one of my own.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I heard he was sick, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't believe he was ill. I hope not. He seemed to be rather wasting away. You shouldn't be doing so much manual labor at that age."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What labor was he doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "He carries stuff around from small tasks to--"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Down at the docks?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The docks, up at the North Ward. He carried crates, boxes. Just odd jobs, ways to get by 'til the end of his days, which now that they've come, may he rest and not have to do odd jobs ever again." He pours his own bit of whiskey.

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: Cheers.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you know who he was doing odd jobs for that day?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Your lady voice is worse than mine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "To be honest, if he did, I wasn't paying attention."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sure his family will miss him very much.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "No, we've been doing repairs on the house and getting everything underway. This is the first thing that's come on this side. Most of them live toward the center of the lake and stay underwater. It's been two in the last few months that have come up. It's a bit of a nuisance."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Damn it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait. None of you look the same.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's right, no, thank you for reminding me. He comes out and he goes, "How can I help you?" (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's us!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's me. I pull the hat off and pull my hair up. It's Beauregard. We killed your snake?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (in normal voice) Pretend that I don't talk-- (in human accent) Pretend that I don't talk like this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fuck it, I drop Disguise Self.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He goes,"Ah!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry to deceive you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'm very confused.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no. Don't worry about it. What was your name, friend?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Rinaldo."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Rinaldo! That's what it was. I knew it was an R! Okay, Rinaldo.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Rinaldo, this snake that was in your house is not the only strange occurrence that's been happening. We're trying to find out the cause of it. Have you seen anything else like this in the time that's passed?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I haven't seen him at the docks in a while. Not for a week, at least."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Have you seen any zombies coming out of the lake?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Zombies? What are you talking about?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're asking after a man that helped down at the docks, named Enon. Have you heard of him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Enon?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Enon.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah, I worked with him a couple times. He comes through, looking for work, asking to see what's available."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You obviously have not heard the news from the traveling circus in town. From last night.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No. That's the big blue tent, right?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, what's going on?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) Enon turned into a zombie.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (louder) Enon turned into a zombie! (whispering) And was killed.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm sorry to hear that. That's terrible. Wow, that's really terrible. I'm sorry."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We were trying to see if he might have worked with people that might have known what he was doing that day, earlier.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Yeah. We don't get many shows and traveling folk. Last time this was here, a year ago, it came and went in a week."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm sorry to hear that. That's terrible. Wow, that's really terrible. I'm sorry."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We were trying to see if he might have worked with people that might have known what he was doing that day, earlier.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I haven't seen him at the docks in a while. Not for a week, at least."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Really?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is there anything out of the ordinary from the past week or two that comes to mind? Anything off about the run-of-the-mill stuff?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Other than a giant fucking snake ruining my house, but not that comes to mind, no. Circus coming into town, that's a little weird."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I see you're skinning the beast. Can I ask why?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm not going to let it go to waste. That's some good meat. Some good skin."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You mentioned the circus coming to town as weird. Why?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Because there's a circus in town."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He just means out of the ordinary, yeah?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You take a couple hours wandering through the Northward, asking questions, and most of the information you get is, they have no idea who that is, or like, "Ah, he died? It's about time."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (singing) 50 feet of hemp rope, it's a standard!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey kiddo, do you want a piggy-back?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure, that sounds like a good idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Scramble up, let's go talk to some people.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, I'll jump on Caleb's back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll wander down to where people are working. Where people are moving crates around.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's on Caleb's back!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, that would be probably closer to the Northward.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so that's the plan, is to look for people moving crap around, the same way that Enon is said to have. So that's what we'll do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Shall I help, or no?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you want to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Not that great.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You aiding him would give him an advantage in the roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh! That didn't help. 12 for me.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. A moment passes and you hear, "Hold on!" The door opens. It was open. There's the sound of heavy sacks go tumbling down to the interior of it and you glance around and you hear a voice go, "Can I help you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are there any windows or anything or is it all a big, dirty, dingy mess?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are windows, but if you look at the back of the windows all you see is scraps of material, stacks of books, piles of boxes and crates, it's literally blocked out with things stacked up against it from the interior.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How busy is the front of this place?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not busy at all, it's just the two of you, right now, standing at the front door.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No one's around us, though?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's not on the main street, it's a little pushed back, recessed about ten or 15 feet from the main road so there's no one immediately around you. But there are people walking back and forth on the road behind but you're out in the open, per se.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And no one's coming to the door?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No one's come to the door yet, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Want me to break in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes. No. [knocking]

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, off the bat, I don't know where that is in D&D Beyond yet. But if I open up my binder I bet I have a paper version that I can verify with. I speak Sylvan, Common, and Celestial and I also can Comprehend Languages as a ritual spell but I think that takes some time.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, so all of you, except for Jester, manage to get a clear view of your vicinity. Jester, you somehow get an eyelash caught in your eye and spend the next hour trying to get it out, but it's in the eyelid.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we fan out a bit? Cover a couple corners, so we can still see each other?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Good idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I'm going to go with Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's not spread out too far, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you two like an item?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You and Fjord, are you like a thing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why, are you interested in him?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, ugh, no!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You travel together.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well yeah, he's a good friend. He watches out for me, I watch out for him, you know. We're a good team. Like you and Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Not at all like that, but yes, all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys fan out a little bit, everyone make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Natural one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't see shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Perception?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ten.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Occasionally, you can hear the crownsguard approaching, and they carry their torches with them. You can see the light crawling forward, so you all duck out of the way and let them pass. About an hour passes. Two hours pass. Around the third hour, you get the sensation that whatever you saw the other night is not arriving on a clockwork time or not arriving at all. It's about three in the morning, in the center of the city by yourself. What do you wish to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I'm going to go with Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's not spread out too far, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you two like an item?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You and Fjord, are you like a thing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why, are you interested in him?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, ugh, no!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You travel together.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well yeah, he's a good friend. He watches out for me, I watch out for him, you know. We're a good team. Like you and Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Not at all like that, but yes, all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys fan out a little bit, everyone make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Natural one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't see shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Perception?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so all of you, except for Jester, manage to get a clear view of your vicinity. Jester, you somehow get an eyelash caught in your eye and spend the next hour trying to get it out, but it's in the eyelid.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Fjord, can you see it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Look up. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To the left.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let me get it--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, no, no. Okay, okay, okay.
